GT LEARN #1463 🍒 Cherry Pickup II HARD DP 3D Memo expected: 15

📝 Problem Statement

Given a rows × cols grid where each cell has cherries (0 or positive). Robot 1 starts at (0,0) and Robot 2 starts at (0,cols-1). Both move simultaneously down by one row; from (r,c) a robot can step to (r+1,c-1), (r+1,c), or (r+1,c+1). When both robots land on the same cell, cherries are counted only once. Return the maximum cherries collectable.

🧠 Solving Approach

Top-down DP with memoization on (r, c1, c2). At each row pick cherries for both robots (deduping if same cell), then explore all 9 combinations of next-step deltas (d1,d2) ∈ {-1,0,1}² and take the max child.

Base / Bound — return 0 when any column is out of [0, cols) or row passes last row.
Transition — solve(r,c1,c2) = cherries(r) + max over 9 children solve(r+1, c1+d1, c2+d2).
Memoization — cache dp[r][c1][c2] so each state computes once.
⏱ O(rows × cols² × 9) 📦 O(rows × cols²)

🧪 Test Cases

T1tiny 2×3→ 15
grid=[[1,2,3],[4,5,6]]
T2LC example 1→ 24
[[3,1,1],[2,5,1],[1,5,5],[2,1,1]]
T32×2 trivial→ 4
grid=[[1,1],[1,1]]
T43×4 sparse→ 12
[[1,0,0,3],[0,0,0,3],[0,0,3,3]]
Live output: —
📜 Python Code
📊 Visualization
0/0 —
←→ 100%
